journeys.name: The Classic Triangle
journeys.tagline: Rome, Florence, Venice — the three cities that define Italy
journeys.intro: This is the Italy you bookmarked. The Colosseum at sunrise, David in the flesh, gondolas under the Bridge of Sighs. Three cities, one perfect arc — connected by high-speed trains and centuries of beauty.

journeys.name: The Grand Tour
journeys.tagline: Rome → Florence → Bologna → Venice — the full Renaissance
journeys.intro: Start where the empire began and end in a floating city. Toss in Bologna — Italy's forgotten food capital — for the best meal of your life. Four cities, four distinct Italys, one incredible journey.

journeys.name: The Amalfi Escape
journeys.tagline: Naples → Amalfi → Rome — coast, culture, and the capital
journeys.intro: Pizza in Naples, lemon groves in Amalfi, and the eternal city to finish. This is the route that does it all — coastal drama and ancient wonders, all in one week.

journeys.name: The Food Trail
journeys.tagline: Bologna → Florence → Turin — eat your way across the north
journeys.intro: Bologna for tortellini, Florence for Chianti, Turin for chocolate and truffles. Three cities, three culinary hearts. This journey is designed for the traveller who eats first and asks questions later.

journeys.name: The Lakes & Alps
journeys.tagline: Milan → Como → Turin → Cinque Terre — mountains, lakes, and the sea
journeys.intro: Lake Como's elegance, the Alps from Turin's hills, and the rugged coast of Cinque Terre. This route is Italy at its most spectacular — every stop is a postcard come to life.

legs.mode: High-speed train to Genoa, then regional

rome.name: Rome

taglines.beach: coast is close but honestly, Rome itself is all you need
taglines.party: Trastevere bars, rooftop aperitivo, night walks past floodlit ruins
taglines.culture: 2000 years of history on every street. The Colosseum. The Vatican. The Sistine Chapel.
taglines.food: the best pasta of your life, then cacio e pepe, then carbonara, then gelato
taglines.relax: afternoon espresso at a pavement cafe, Villa Borghese at golden hour
taglines.mix: Rome is not a city. It is a beautiful, chaotic, 2000-year-old masterpiece.

rome.welcome: Rome is not a city you will visit. It is a city you will experience. From the ancient grandeur of the Colosseum to the quiet magic of Trastevere's cobbled streets, every corner tells a story. The pasta is better than you imagined, the coffee is life-changing, and the chaos somehow works. Give yourself time to wander aimlessly - that is where Rome truly reveals itself.

budget.name: The RomeHello Hostel
budget.desc: A vibrant, community-focused hostel with striking murals, a lively bar/grill, cooking classes, and free walking tours. Beautifully restored building near Termini.
budget.proximity: 3 min walk to Repubblica Metro, 10 min to Trevi Fountain

budget.name: Generator Rome
budget.desc: Design-led hostel-hotel hybrid with a buzzing rooftop bar serving Aperol Spritzes. Stylish and affordable with dorms and private rooms.
budget.proximity: 2 min to Vittorio Emanuele Metro, 15 min to Colosseum

budget.name: Hotel Katty
budget.desc: Friendly, clean budget hotel on the 5th floor of a noble palace with a charming vintage elevator. Family-run with spacious rooms.
budget.proximity: 2 min to Castro Pretorio Metro, 20 min walk to Trevi Fountain

mid.name: Hotel Artemide
mid.desc: Elegant 4-star with a rooftop restaurant, full spa, and free minibar in every room. Housed in a 19th-century building with refined modern interiors.
mid.proximity: 2 min to Repubblica Metro, 10 min walk to Trevi Fountain

mid.name: Hotel Colosseum
mid.desc: Family-run 3-star since 1972 in the charming Monti district. Rooftop terrace with panoramic views over ancient Rome.
mid.proximity: 5 min walk to Colosseum, 8 min to Cavour Metro

mid.name: Nerva Boutique Hotel
mid.desc: Chic 20-room boutique facing the Imperial Forums with sleek modern Italian design and handmade breakfast. Exceptional service — location score 9.7.
mid.proximity: 3 min to Colosseum, 5 min to Roman Forum

luxury.name: Hotel de Russie
luxury.desc: Legendary Rocco Forte 5-star with a secret terraced garden designed by Valadier. Classical elegance meets cosmopolitan flair at the Stravinsky Bar.
luxury.proximity: 5 min to Spanish Steps, 2 min to Flaminio Metro

luxury.name: The St. Regis Rome
luxury.desc: The first St. Regis in Europe (1894) in a magnificent palazzo. Opulent interiors with Rubelli silks, Murano glass, and signature butler service.
luxury.proximity: 4 min to Repubblica Metro, 10 min to Trevi Fountain

luxury.name: Hotel Eden
luxury.desc: Dorchester Collection 5-star icon atop Via Veneto with breathtaking views from Michelin-starred rooftop La Terrazza. A favourite of royalty since 1889.
luxury.proximity: 5 min to Spanish Steps, 8 min to Barberini Metro

donts: Don't eat at a restaurant with pictures on the menu - you're in a tourist trap
Don't visit the Vatican without booking tickets in advance - the queue wraps around the walls
Don't throw coins into more than one fountain - it's not a wishing well, it's littering
Don't order cappuccino after 11am - Italians consider this a crime against breakfast
Don't assume all taxis are metered - agree the price before you get in
rome.transport: Rome is best explored on foot. The Metro has two main lines (A and B) that cover most attractions. Buses fill the gaps. Taxis are white and official only. The Hop-On Hop-Off bus is actually useful here for the first day. Buy a 48-hour Roma Pass for unlimited transport and museum discounts.

fiestas.desc: the Pope leads mass in St. Peter's Square, the whole city fills with pilgrims.
free concerts, cinema screenings, and outdoor events all summer.
the city's birthday celebrated with parades and fireworks.

phrases.meaning: Good morning - use until early afternoon

attractions.name: Colosseum and Roman Forum
attractions.desc: Book the underground tour for access to the gladiator tunnels. Mind-blowing.

attractions.name: Vatican Museums and Sistine Chapel
attractions.desc: Book the earliest slot. The Sistine Chapel ceiling is genuinely overwhelming.

attractions.name: Trevi Fountain
attractions.desc: Go at 6am to see it without crowds. Magical.

attractions.name: Trastevere
attractions.desc: Best neighbourhood for evening walks and authentic Roman dinner.

attractions.name: Pantheon
attractions.desc: The best-preserved ancient building in Rome. Free to enter.

attractions.name: Villa Borghese
attractions.desc: Galleria Borghese requires booking but is worth every second.

attractions.name: Spanish Steps
attractions.desc: Go at sunset for the view over the city.

attractions.name: Catacombs of San Callisto
attractions.desc: Underground burial chambers. Eerie and fascinating.

day_trips.name: Ostia Antica
day_trips.tip: Like Pompeii but closer and far less crowded

day_trips.name: Tivoli and Villa d'Este
day_trips.tip: Renaissance villa with the most incredible fountains

day_trips.name: Castel Gandolfo
day_trips.tip: The Pope's summer residence on a crater lake

sarah_knows.to_city: Leonardo Express train to Termini (€14, 32min). Taxi (€50 fixed, 45min). Bus Terravision (€6, 50min)
sarah_knows.best_time: April-June or September-October for perfect weather and manageable crowds
eating: Carbonara is made with egg, pecorino, guanciale, and black pepper. NEVER cream. If a restaurant uses cream, leave.
Dinner starts at 8pm at the earliest, 9pm is normal. Lunch is 1-3pm, not earlier.
Gelato from a shop with muted, natural colours - bright colours mean artificial flavouring, avoid it
Pasta is a first course (primo), not a main. Follow it with a second course (secondo) like real Romans.
safety: Pickpockets are everywhere on the Metro, especially Line A. Bag in front, phone away.
The area around Termini station is safe by day but less comfortable after 10pm
Emergency: 112 (EU-wide). Rome police: 113
Watch the 'free rose' scam - someone hands you a rose then demands payment
local_tips: Book Colosseum and Vatican tickets WEEKS in advance - they sell out daily
The 'tap water is bad' myth - it's perfectly safe. Fill your bottle at public nasoni fountains for free
Rome is cobblestone hell - wear flat, comfortable shoes. Heels will destroy you
Many restaurants charge 'coperto' (cover charge) of €1-3 per person - it's normal, not a scam

airports.name: Leonardo da Vinci-Fiumicino
airports.train: Leonardo Express every 15-30 min, £14, 32 min non-stop to Termini

airport.tips: Only use official white taxis with rooftop TAXI sign and Comune di Roma crest. FCO to centre flat rate £50. Ignore touts. Pre-book airport bus tickets online for discounts.

attractionPrices.name: Colosseum, Forum & Palatine (combined)
attractionPrices.notes: Booking essential (skip-queue). Free 1st Sun of month. Queue 1-3hrs peak.

attractionPrices.name: Vatican Museums & Sistine Chapel
attractionPrices.notes: £20 + £5 booking fee. Free last Sun of month. Queue 1-3hrs.

attractionPrices.name: St. Peter's Basilica
attractionPrices.notes: Free entry. Dome climb £10 (lift+320 steps). Dress code enforced.

attractionPrices.name: Galleria Borghese
attractionPrices.notes: Booking mandatory (2hr timed slots). Free 1st Sun. Closed Mon.

attractionPrices.name: Pantheon
attractionPrices.notes: £5 adults, £2 under 25. Free 1st Sun. Queue 15-30 min.

attractionPrices.name: Trevi Fountain
attractionPrices.notes: Always free. Best at dawn or after midnight.

attractionPrices.name: Castel Sant'Angelo
attractionPrices.notes: £16 adults. Free 1st Sun. Booking recommended.

attractionPrices.name: Capitoline Museums
attractionPrices.notes: £16 adults. Free 1st Sun. Closed Mon.

dayTrips.name: Tivoli
dayTrips.description: Villa d'Este (Renaissance fountains), Hadrian's Villa (imperial ruins). Combined ticket £18.
dayTrips.transport: Cotral bus from Ponte Mammolo (£3.20, 1-1.5h) or FL2 train from Tiburtina (£2.60, 1h).

dayTrips.name: Ostia Antica
dayTrips.description: Ancient Roman port city with theatre, baths, mosaics. Entry £18.
dayTrips.transport: Metro B to Piramide, then Roma-Lido train to Ostia Antica (30 min, £1.50).

dayTrips.name: Pompeii
dayTrips.description: Forum, amphitheatre, plaster casts. Entry £23. Full day trip.
dayTrips.transport: High-speed train Termini to Napoli (70 min, £25-45) + Circumvesuviana to Pompei.

dayTrips.name: Orvieto
dayTrips.description: Duomo, underground caves, St. Patrick's Well. Carta Unica pass ~£20.
dayTrips.transport: Direct train from Termini (70-90 min, £7-15) + funicular.

dayTrips.name: Castel Gandolfo
dayTrips.description: Papal Palace museum (£12), Lake Albano swimming.
dayTrips.transport: Regional train from Termini (45 min, £2.10) + 15-min walk.

florence.name: Florence

taglines.beach: landlocked - the Renaissance city doesn't need a beach
taglines.party: wine windows, rooftop bars, respectable but not wild
taglines.culture: the Uffizi, the Duomo, Michelangelo's David. Art overload in the best way.
taglines.food: Tuscan steak, lampredotto, gelato that ruins all other gelato for you
taglines.relax: Boboli Gardens, afternoon wine, watching the sunset from Piazzale Michelangelo
taglines.mix: Florence is a living museum, and somehow it still feels like home

florence.welcome: Florence is the Renaissance's greatest gift to the modern world. In no other city can you turn a corner and find yourself face-to-face with Michelangelo's David, Brunelleschi's Duomo, or Botticelli's Venus. The art is world-class, but so is the food - Florentine steak and fresh pasta will haunt your dreams. It is small, walkable, and utterly magical. Prepare to fall in love.

budget.name: Plus Florence Hostel
budget.desc: Large, modern hostel with two swimming pools, spa, gym and rooftop bar — more like a resort. Popular with backpackers and groups.
budget.proximity: 15-20 min walk to Duomo, 10 min to SMN station

budget.name: Hostel Santa Monaca
budget.desc: Historic hostel in a former 15th-century monastery in the lively Oltrarno district. Simple, authentic, pilgrim-house feel.
budget.proximity: 15 min walk to Duomo, 10 min to Ponte Vecchio

budget.name: Hotel Centro
budget.desc: Charming 2-star in a restored Renaissance palazzo that once housed Raphael. Classic Florentine style with frescoed ceilings.
budget.proximity: 5 min to Duomo, 5 min to Accademia

mid.name: Hotel La Scaletta
mid.desc: Charming hotel in a 15th-century building between Ponte Vecchio and Palazzo Pitti. Rooftop terrace with panoramic Florence views.
mid.proximity: 1 min to Ponte Vecchio, 10 min to Duomo

mid.name: Hotel Davanzati
mid.desc: Family-run 3-star with exceptional hospitality. Guests rave about the staff, happy hour and breakfast in a historic building.
mid.proximity: 5 min to Uffizi, 7 min to Duomo

mid.name: Hotel Casci
mid.desc: Simple 2-star family-run in a 15th-century building with original frescoes. Basic but clean with a warm welcome.
mid.proximity: 5 min to Accademia, 10 min to Duomo

luxury.name: Hotel Brunelleschi
luxury.desc: Luxury 4-star in a restored Byzantine tower and medieval church right on the Duomo piazza. Elegant rooms with impeccable service.
luxury.proximity: 30 sec to Duomo, 5 min to Uffizi

luxury.name: Four Seasons Florence
luxury.desc: Ultra-luxury 5-star in a 15th-century palazzo with 11 acres of private gardens, Michelin-star restaurant, and world-class spa.
luxury.proximity: 15 min walk to Duomo, 10 min to Accademia

luxury.name: Helvetia and Bristol
luxury.desc: Historic 5-star in a 19th-century palazzo on one of Florence's prettiest squares. Old-world charm with antique furniture and a famed bar.
luxury.proximity: 5 min to Duomo, 5 min to Ponte Vecchio

donts: Don't queue for the Uffizi or Accademia without pre-booked tickets - you'll wait 3+ hours
Don't take photos inside the Sistine Chapel or the Uffizi galleries
Don't eat at a restaurant with a 'tourist menu' in multiple languages - walk away
Don't climb the Duomo if you're claustrophobic - the staircase is narrow and unrelenting
florence.transport: Florence is perfectly walkable - the historic centre is compact and mostly car-free. Buses connect the suburbs but you probably won't need them. Taxis from the train station. Walk everywhere - that's the point of Florence.

fiestas.desc: an explosive cart parade where a dove-shaped rocket lights a cart of fireworks.
a brutal historical football match in medieval costume.
fireworks over the Arno.

attractions.name: Duomo and Brunelleschi's Dome
attractions.desc: Book the climb. The view over Florence is worth every step.

attractions.name: Uffizi Gallery
attractions.desc: Botticelli, Leonardo, Raphael. Book weeks ahead. Give it 3 hours.

attractions.name: Galleria dell'Accademia
attractions.desc: Michelangelo's David. You will cry. That's normal.

attractions.name: Ponte Vecchio
attractions.desc: The bridge with shops built into it. Go at sunrise for photos.

attractions.name: Boboli Gardens
attractions.desc: Renaissance gardens behind the Pitti Palace. Peaceful afternoon escape.

attractions.name: Piazzale Michelangelo
attractions.desc: Sunset viewpoint over the whole city. Bring wine.

attractions.name: Santa Croce Basilica
attractions.desc: Burial place of Michelangelo, Galileo, and Machiavelli.

attractions.name: Mercato Centrale
attractions.desc: Eat at the upstairs food hall. The truffle pasta is extraordinary.

day_trips.name: Fiesole
day_trips.tip: Ancient Roman ruins and views over Florence from the hills

day_trips.name: Siena
day_trips.tip: Medieval rival city. The Piazza del Campo is one of Europe's great squares

day_trips.name: San Gimignano
day_trips.tip: The city of towers - 14 medieval skyscrapers still standing

day_trips.name: Chianti Wine Region
day_trips.tip: The classic Tuscany day out - castles, vineyards, olive groves

airports.name: Florence Peretola
airports.buses: No direct bus; tram T2 to city centre
airports.train: No train; tram T2 to SMN station

airport.tips: FLR tram T2 runs 5:00-00:30 (Fri-Sat until 02:00), every 5-18 min, €2. Pisa train is fastest value option; book regional tickets on the day.

attractionPrices.name: Duomo Complex
attractionPrices.notes: Cathedral free. Brunelleschi Pass €30 (Dome, Bell Tower, Baptistery, Museum, Crypt) valid 72h. Book dome climb time slot online.

attractionPrices.name: Uffizi Gallery
attractionPrices.notes: €29 online, €25 on-site. Free 1st Sun monthly. Closed Mondays. Book timed slot 2+ weeks ahead.

attractionPrices.name: Accademia Gallery (David)
attractionPrices.notes: €16 on-site. Free 1st Sun monthly. Closed Mondays. Must book in peak season.

attractionPrices.name: Palazzo Vecchio
attractionPrices.notes: €19 full (museum + tower). Free 1st Sun monthly. Under-18 free.

attractionPrices.name: Boboli Gardens
attractionPrices.notes: €13 online, €10 on-site. Free 1st Sun monthly. Combined with Pitti Palace €22.

attractionPrices.name: Ponte Vecchio
attractionPrices.notes: Free, always open. Best at sunset.

attractionPrices.name: Piazzale Michelangelo
attractionPrices.notes: Free panoramic viewpoint. Best at sunset.

dayTrips.name: Siena
dayTrips.description: UNESCO medieval city with shell-shaped Piazza del Campo, Gothic Duomo, Palio horse race.
dayTrips.transport: Train from SMN ~1.5h, €10-15; bus ~1h 15min, €7-10

dayTrips.name: Pisa
dayTrips.description: Leaning Tower, Piazza dei Miracoli, Cathedral, Baptistery. Easy half-day.
dayTrips.transport: Train from SMN ~1h, €8-10, up to 47 trains/day. 20 min walk to tower.

dayTrips.name: San Gimignano
dayTrips.description: Hilltop medieval town with 14 surviving tower-houses, gelato shops, panoramic views.
dayTrips.transport: Bus to Poggibonsi (1h, €5-8) then local bus 130 (15 min)

dayTrips.name: Chianti
dayTrips.description: Wine region with rolling vineyards, olive groves, medieval castles, wine tasting.
dayTrips.transport: Best by organised tour (~€49-69) or car. Bus 365 from Florence ~1h.

dayTrips.name: Fiesole
dayTrips.description: Ancient Etruscan-Roman hill town with ruins, cathedral, panoramic Florence views.
dayTrips.transport: Bus #7 from Piazza San Marco ~25-30 min, €1.70, every 20 min

sarah_knows.to_city: Tram T2 from airport to Santa Maria Novella station (€1.70, 22min). Or Volainbus shuttle (€6, 25min). Taxi (€25 fixed, 20min)
sarah_knows.best_time: April-June or September-October. July-August is packed and painfully hot
eating: Bistecca alla Fiorentina is a T-bone steak, rare-cooked. Order it for two at minimum
Lampredotto is a street food sandwich of cow's fourth stomach - try it before you say no
Gelato at Gelateria dei Neri is the best in Florence. Queue is worth it
Chianti Classico from a black rooster bottle is the real deal
safety: Pickpockets around the Duomo and Uffizi - bag in front always
The area around Santa Maria Novella station is fine by day, less comfortable after dark
Emergency: 112 (EU-wide)
local_tips: Book Uffizi and Accademia tickets a MONTH in advance during high season
The Firenze Card is worth it if you plan 5+ museums in 72 hours
Climb Giotto's Campanile instead of the Duomo - shorter queue, better view
Visit the 'wine windows' (buchette del vino) - tiny hatches where wine was sold during plague

venice.name: Venice

taglines.beach: the whole city is a lagoon - beaches at Lido a 10-min boat ride
taglines.party: carnival, aperitivo hour, jazz bars in Dorsoduro
taglines.culture: St. Mark's, Doge's Palace, the Biennale - Venice is one giant masterpiece
taglines.food: cicchetti, fresh lagoon seafood, the best tiramisu of your life
taglines.relax: gondola rides are touristy, but also genuinely magical
taglines.mix: Venice is impossible. Impossible to describe, impossible to forget.

venice.welcome: Venice is a dream made real. Built on water, held up by wooden piles driven into the lagoon floor over a thousand years ago, it should not exist - and yet here it is, impossibly beautiful. St. Mark's Square, the Grand Canal, the quiet backstreets with laundry hanging overhead. Getting lost is the point. Every corner reveals something extraordinary.

budget.name: Generator Venice
budget.desc: Stylish hostel on Giudecca Island in a converted warehouse with mosaic floors and Grand Canal views. Popular with younger travellers.
budget.proximity: 10-min vaporetto to St Mark's, 10-min to Rialto

budget.name: Ostello Venezia
budget.desc: Original HI hostel on Giudecca Island with dorms and private rooms across from St Mark's. Clean, no-frills with lively common area.

budget.name: Hotel Tintoretto
budget.desc: Characteristic 3-star in Cannaregio with elegant en-suite rooms, warm colours and canal views. On-site restaurant with regional cuisine.
budget.proximity: 5-min walk to Rialto, 15-min to St Mark's

mid.name: Hotel San Cassiano Ca' Favretto
mid.desc: Elegant 4-star in a 14th-century palazzo with Grand Canal views. Antique furniture, Murano chandeliers and period charm.
mid.proximity: 8-min walk to Rialto, 15-min to St Mark's

mid.name: Ca' dei Dogi
mid.desc: Charming guesthouse in a historic palace next to the Doge's Palace. Rooftop terrace overlooking palace spires and Bridge of Sighs.
mid.proximity: 2-min walk to Doge's Palace, 4-min to St Mark's

mid.name: Hotel Palazzo Barbarigo
mid.desc: Boutique 4-star in a 16th-century palazzo on the Grand Canal with original frescoes. Winner of Italian TV show 4 Hotels.
mid.proximity: On Grand Canal, 10-min walk to Rialto

luxury.name: Hotel Danieli
luxury.desc: Legendary 5-star (Four Seasons) in three interconnecting 15th-century palaces. Ornate Venetian architecture and famed rooftop restaurant.

luxury.name: Ca' Maria Adele
luxury.desc: Intimate 12-room boutique beside Basilica della Salute, named most romantic boutique hotel in the world in 2014.

luxury.name: Palazzo Venart Luxury Hotel
luxury.desc: Opulent 5-star in a restored 16th-century palace on the Grand Canal. Michelin-starred Glam restaurant.
luxury.proximity: 10-min walk to Rialto, 10-15 min to St Mark's

donts: Don't eat at the tables on St. Mark's Square - €50 for a spag bol is not a flex
Don't take a gondola from the main tourist spots - walk a few streets back for half the price
Don't touch the water - it will ruin your leather shoes permanently
Don't try to drive to Venice - leave the car in Mestre and take the train over the bridge
Don't expect to see everything in a weekend - Venice rewards slow exploration
venice.transport: Venice has no cars. Walk or take the vaporetto (water bus). The vaporetto system is the public transport - buy a 24/48/72-hour pass. Water taxis are fast but very expensive. Traghetti (tiny gondola ferries) cross the Grand Canal for €2.

fiestas.desc: the most famous masquerade ball on earth.
pilgrims cross a temporary bridge to the Salute church.
historical gondola parade and races on the Grand Canal.

phrases.phrase: Quanto costa un giro in gondola?
phrases.meaning: How much is a gondola ride?

attractions.name: St. Mark's Basilica
attractions.desc: Byzantine gold mosaics that cover every surface. Go at opening.

attractions.name: Doge's Palace
attractions.desc: The seat of Venetian power. The Bridge of Sighs connects to the prison.

attractions.name: Grand Canal
attractions.desc: Take vaporetto line 1 from one end to the other - the cheapest sightseeing tour.

attractions.name: Rialto Bridge and Market
attractions.desc: Early morning market is best. Go before 9am.

attractions.name: Peggy Guggenheim Collection
attractions.desc: 20th century art in a palazzo on the Grand Canal.

attractions.name: Burano Island
attractions.desc: Colourful fishermen's houses. A photographer's dream. 40-min vaporetto.

attractions.name: Murano Island
attractions.desc: Glass-blowing workshops. Watch masters at work. Free demos.

attractions.name: Dorsoduro
attractions.desc: Quietest sestiere. Best for genuine local bars and restaurants.

day_trips.name: Burano and Murano
day_trips.tip: Go early - Burano is a photographer's paradise before the crowds

day_trips.name: Lido di Venezia
day_trips.tip: Venice's beach island. Peaceful, sandy, good for an escape

day_trips.name: Padua (Padova)
day_trips.tip: Giotto's Scrovegni Chapel frescoes are among the most important paintings in history

day_trips.name: Verona
day_trips.tip: Romeo and Juliet's balcony, Roman arena, beautiful city less than 2 hours away

airports.name: Venice Marco Polo
airports.buses: Alilaguna water bus €18 (60-90 min); ACTV Aerobus €10 (25 min to Piazzale Roma); ATVO Express €12 (20 min to Piazzale Roma)
airports.train: No direct train. Bus to Mestre station then train to Santa Lucia (~€1.50 + €10 bus)
airports.taxi: Private water taxi €110-130 (30 min, up to 5 pax); shared water taxi ~€30-39 pp

airports.name: Treviso
airports.buses: ATVO express bus €14 (60-70 min to Piazzale Roma); Barzi bus €10 (40 min to Tronchetto)
airports.train: Bus line 6 to Treviso station (€2-3, 15 min) then train to Santa Lucia (€3-5, 30-40 min)

airport.tips: Buy Alilaguna tickets online or at airport kiosk (not ACTV machines). ACTV Aerobus cheaper but stop at Piazzale Roma only. 24h ACTV pass (€25) worth it if using vaporetto same day.

attractionPrices.name: St Mark's Basilica
attractionPrices.notes: Basic €10. Full ticket (incl. Pala d'Oro + Museum) €30. Campanile €15. Skip-the-line €8.

attractionPrices.name: Doge's Palace
attractionPrices.notes: Combo ticket includes Correr Museum, Archaeological Museum & Marciana Library. Free 1st Sun.

attractionPrices.name: Gondola ride
attractionPrices.notes: €90 per boat (up to 5) for 30 min daytime. €110 for 35 min evening. Fixed city-wide rates.

attractionPrices.name: Peggy Guggenheim Collection
attractionPrices.notes: €17 adults, €14 seniors 70+, €9 students. Closed Tuesdays.

attractionPrices.name: Ca' Rezzonico
attractionPrices.notes: €15 adults, €7.50 reduced. Part of MUVE Museum Pass (€50 for 11 museums).

attractionPrices.name: Scuola Grande di San Rocco
attractionPrices.notes: €10 adults, €8 reduced. Audio guide included. Buy at door only.

dayTrips.name: Murano
dayTrips.description: Glass-blowing island, Museo del Vetro (€15), authentic Murano glass shopping.
dayTrips.transport: Vaporetto Line 12 from Fondamente Nove, 15 min, €9.50 single or €25 24h pass

dayTrips.name: Burano
dayTrips.description: Rainbow-coloured fishermen's island, lace-making museum (€5), extremely photogenic.
dayTrips.transport: Vaporetto Line 12 from Fondamente Nove (45 min) or from Murano (30 min)

dayTrips.name: Torcello
dayTrips.description: Near-deserted island with 12th-century Byzantine cathedral (€5), peaceful atmosphere.
dayTrips.transport: Vaporetto Line 12 from Burano (5 min). Best after Murano + Burano.

dayTrips.name: Padua
dayTrips.description: Giotto's Scrovegni Chapel frescoes (UNESCO), world's oldest botanical garden.
dayTrips.transport: Regional train from Santa Lucia, 25-35 min, €4-9.50 each way, every 15-30 min

dayTrips.name: Verona
dayTrips.description: Roman Arena (opera venue), Juliet's Balcony, Piazza delle Erbe, great wine.
dayTrips.transport: Regional/high-speed train from Santa Lucia, 1-1.5 hr, €8-15 each way

sarah_knows.airport: Marco Polo Airport (VCE) or Treviso Airport (TSF for Ryanair)
sarah_knows.to_city: Alilaguna water bus from Marco Polo to St. Mark's (€15, 1hr). ATVO bus to Piazzale Roma (€8, 20min). Water taxi (€100+, 20min)
sarah_knows.best_time: March-May or September-October. Avoid November-February (acqua alta flooding) and July-August (crowded, hot, smelly)
eating: Cicchetti are Venetian tapas - small plates, €2-4 each, eaten standing at a bar
Spritz (Aperol or Select) is the local drink. It was invented here, not in Milan
Sarde in saor (sweet and sour sardines) is the most authentic Venetian dish
Restaurants near Rialto and St. Mark's are worse and more expensive - walk 10 minutes away
safety: Venice is very safe but water levels (acqua alta) can make walking dangerous - check the tide forecast app
Pickpockets on vaporettos during rush hour - bag zipped and in front
Emergency: 112 (EU-wide)
local_tips: Buy a vaporetto pass - single tickets are €9.50, a 72-hour pass is €45
The Ghetto is the oldest Jewish quarter in the world - worth a visit
Venice isn't an amusement park, it's a living city with 50,000 residents - be respectful
Book everything in advance during Carnival - accommodation triples in price

milan.name: Milan

taglines.beach: landlocked - but Lake Como is an hour north
taglines.party: designer clubs, fashion weeks, aperitivo culture that defines Italian nightlife
taglines.culture: The Last Supper, La Scala, the Duomo. High culture at its highest.
taglines.food: risotto alla Milanese, cotoletta, panettone. The food capital of the north.
taglines.relax: Brera district, art galleries, spritz at sunset
taglines.mix: Milan is Italy's only real metropolis - business, fashion, and extraordinary culture

milan.welcome: Milan is Italy's engine - the fashion capital, design capital, and financial heart of the country. But don't let the skyscrapers fool you, the city has a soul. Leonardo's Last Supper hides in a convent wall. La Scala is the most famous opera house in the world. The Duomo took 600 years to build and it shows. Milan is fast, stylish, and utterly captivating.

budget.name: Ostello Bello Grande
budget.desc: High-energy hostel steps from Centrale with daily karaoke, pub crawls, free pasta kitchen and 24hr bar.
budget.proximity: 50m from Milano Centrale, 25-min walk to Duomo

budget.name: Carlo Goldoni Hotel
budget.desc: Simple 2-star family-run hotel near Dateo station. Soundproofed rooms with AC, free WiFi, terrace and bar.

budget.name: NYX Hotel Milan
budget.desc: Trendy art-driven hotel with street-art murals, DJ nights and vintage video games. Rooftop terrace overlooking the skyline.
budget.proximity: Next to Centrale station, 15-min metro to Duomo

mid.name: Room Mate Giulia
mid.desc: Boutique design hotel in a 19th-century building with vintage-themed interiors. Calm and refined with fitness centre and sauna.

mid.name: Hotel Spadari al Duomo
mid.desc: Elegant 4-star filled with contemporary artwork. Exceptional service and American buffet breakfast.

mid.name: NH Collection Milano President
mid.desc: Sleek 5-star near San Babila with 274 rooms. Polished Italian design and excellent restaurant.

luxury.name: Armani Hotel Milano
luxury.desc: Minimalist luxury by Giorgio Armani. Indoor pool, Armani/SPA with panoramic views, sophisticated restaurant.

luxury.name: Four Seasons Milan
luxury.desc: Ultra-luxury in a converted 15th-century convent with frescoed ceilings, garden courtyard and world-class spa.

luxury.name: Mandarin Oriental Milan
luxury.desc: Chic 5-star with courtyard, Michelin-starred dining and serene spa. Milanese elegance meets Oriental luxury.

donts: Don't order a cappuccino after 11am - you'll get a lecture from every Italian in the room
Don't expect the city to be as immediately beautiful as Rome or Florence - Milan reveals itself slowly
Don't skip the happy hour (aperitivo) - this is where Milan comes alive
Don't take a photo of The Last Supper without booking tickets months in advance - you won't get in
Don't call panettone a cake - it's panettone. And it originated here.
milan.transport: The Metro is excellent, clean, and covers the whole city. A 24-hour ticket costs €4.50 and is incredible value. Trams are historic and charming. The city is walkable but big - use the Metro between districts. Taxis are metered and honest.

fiestas.desc: the city transforms. Book everything far in advance.
design week that takes over the entire city.
canal festival with markets and concerts.

phrases.meaning: Formal hello - common in Milan more than 'ciao'

attractions.name: Duomo di Milano
attractions.desc: Go to the roof. The city's best view. 600 years of construction.

attractions.name: The Last Supper (Cenacolo Vinciano)
attractions.desc: Book months ahead. 15-minute viewing slots. Absolutely worth it.

attractions.name: La Scala Opera House
attractions.desc: Tour the museum and theatre. Book a performance if you can.

attractions.name: Galleria Vittorio Emanuele II
attractions.desc: The world's oldest shopping mall. Spin on the bull's testicles for luck.

attractions.name: Sforza Castle
attractions.desc: Michelangelo's last sculpture is inside. Free entry to the grounds.

attractions.name: Brera District
attractions.desc: Milan's artsy neighbourhood. Galleries, boutiques, best aperitivo bars.

attractions.name: Navigli District
attractions.desc: The canal district. Best nightlife and restaurant scene in Milan.

attractions.name: Pinacoteca di Brera
attractions.desc: Raphael, Caravaggio, Bellini. World-class collection.

day_trips.name: Lake Como
day_trips.tip: Bellagio is the most beautiful town. Take the ferry between villages.

day_trips.name: Bergamo
day_trips.tip: Citta Alta (upper town) is medieval perfection. 50 minutes by train.

day_trips.name: Pavia and Certosa
day_trips.tip: Renaissance monastery of Certosa di Pavia is one of Italy's hidden gems

airports.name: Milan Malpensa
airports.buses: Terravision/Malpensa Shuttle €10, 50 min to Milano Centrale, every 20-30 min
airports.train: Malpensa Express €15, 50 min to Cadorna/Centrale, every 30 min

airports.name: Milan Linate
airports.buses: Starfly/Air Bus 73 €5-7, 25 min to Milano Centrale, every 20-30 min
airports.train: Metro M4 €2.20, 12 min to San Babila, every 5 min

airports.name: Bergamo Orio al Serio
airports.buses: Orio Shuttle/Terravision/Flixbus €10-12, 50 min to Milano Centrale, every 30 min
airports.train: No direct rail; bus to Bergamo station then train (€7-10, ~1.5h total)

airport.tips: MXP is main hub furthest out (50km). LIN is closest (7km) with fast M4 metro. BGY serves budget airlines; Orio Shuttle is best value.

attractionPrices.name: Duomo di Milano
attractionPrices.notes: +Rooftop stairs €26; +elevator €32. Free 1st Sun. Book rooftop time slot online.

attractionPrices.name: The Last Supper
attractionPrices.notes: Mandatory advance booking — sells out weeks ahead. 15-min timed slots. Closed Mondays.

attractionPrices.name: Sforza Castle
attractionPrices.notes: Grounds free. Museums €10 full. Free 1st & 3rd Tue 2pm, 1st Sun. Houses Michelangelo's Pietà Rondanini.

attractionPrices.name: Brera Gallery
attractionPrices.notes: EU 18-25 €2. Free 1st Sun (reservation required). Closed Mondays. Raphael, Mantegna, Caravaggio.

attractionPrices.name: La Scala Museum
attractionPrices.notes: Reduced €8 (6-18yr, 65+, students). Includes theatre auditorium view (if no rehearsal).

attractionPrices.name: Galleria Vittorio Emanuele II
attractionPrices.notes: Free entry, open 24/7. Historic arcade with luxury boutiques.

attractionPrices.name: Navigli District
attractionPrices.notes: Free canal district. Best at evening for aperitivo.

dayTrips.name: Lake Como
dayTrips.description: Alpine lake with elegant villas, Bellagio, Varenna, boat ferries.
dayTrips.transport: Train Milano Centrale to Como S. Giovanni (35-40 min, €5) or Varenna (60 min, €8). Ferries €5-15.

dayTrips.name: Bergamo
dayTrips.description: Medieval hilltop Città Alta, funicular, Venetian walls (UNESCO), Piazza Vecchia.
dayTrips.transport: Train Milano Centrale to Bergamo (50 min, €5-7). Funicular to Città Alta €1.30.

dayTrips.name: Pavia
dayTrips.description: University city with Certosa di Pavia monastery, Visconti Castle, Romanesque churches.
dayTrips.transport: Train Milano Centrale/Rogoredo to Pavia (20-30 min, €5)

dayTrips.name: Verona
dayTrips.description: Juliet's balcony, Roman Arena (opera venue), Piazza delle Erbe, Ponte Pietra.
dayTrips.transport: High-speed train Milano Centrale to Verona Porta Nuova (1h 20min, €15-25)

dayTrips.name: Turin
dayTrips.description: Egyptian Museum, Mole Antonelliana, Savoy palaces, chocolate culture.
dayTrips.transport: High-speed train Milano Centrale to Torino Porta Nuova (1h, €15-30)

sarah_knows.airport: Malpensa Airport (MXP) - main. Linate (LIN) - closer. Bergamo (BGY) - Ryanair
sarah_knows.to_city: Malpensa Express train to Cadorna/Centrale (€13, 50min). Bus to Centrale (€10, 50min). Taxi from Malpensa (€95 fixed, 45min)
sarah_knows.best_time: April-June or September-October. Skip August (city empties out, many places closed)
eating: Risotto alla Milanese is saffron risotto - the golden colour comes from real saffron, not turmeric
Cotoletta alla Milanese is a breaded veal chop, fried in butter - bigger and better than a schnitzel
Panettone was invented in Milan - buy it at Pasticceria Marchesi for the real version
Aperitivo in Milan means a buffet of food included with your drink - skip dinner and do this instead
safety: Milan is generally safe but Central Station and the Navigli area at night require awareness
Pickpockets on the Metro (especially M1, M3 lines) - keep bags zipped
Emergency: 112 (EU-wide)
local_tips: Book The Last Supper at least 2 months ahead - tickets release monthly and sell out within hours
Duomo roof tickets are a separate purchase from the cathedral entry
Milan is a walking city for the centre, but use Metro for Porta Nuova and Navigli
Fashion Week means hotel prices triple - avoid if you're not here for fashion

naples.name: Naples

taglines.beach: Posillipo, Sorrento coast, the islands - Naples is the gateway
taglines.party: chaotic, loud, brilliant. Naples parties like it has nothing to lose.
taglines.culture: Pompeii, Herculaneum, the National Museum. History is everywhere here.
taglines.food: pizza. The best pizza on earth. Then sfogliatella, then more pizza.
taglines.relax: there's no such thing as a quiet Naples day - and that's the point
taglines.mix: Naples is chaos, crime, and the most beautiful street food in Europe

naples.welcome: Naples is the most misunderstood city in Italy, and the most rewarding. It is chaotic, loud, and at times overwhelming - but it is also home to the best pizza on Earth, the most important archaeological museum in Italy, and Pompeii is practically a suburb. Neapolitans are the warmest, funniest people you will meet. Surrender to the chaos. You will leave changed.

budget.name: Hostel of the Sun
budget.desc: Award-winning hostel near the ferry terminal. Social bar, shared lounge and daily activities.

budget.name: Six Small Rooms
budget.desc: Cozy hostel with shared kitchen, games room and lounge. Clean rooms with AC and free WiFi.

budget.name: Hotel Potenza
budget.desc: Recently refurbished 3-star near the main station. Practical with restaurant, bar and English-speaking staff.
budget.proximity: Near Napoli Centrale, 30-min walk to centre

mid.name: Hotel Piazza Bellini
mid.desc: Tranquil retreat with peaceful garden and library. Excellent breakfast in the ancient streets.

mid.name: Costantinopoli 104
mid.desc: Elegant 4-star villa with gorgeous garden, outdoor pool and Liberty-style stained glass. Rooftop terrace.

mid.name: Hotel Il Convento
mid.desc: Charming hotel in a converted convent near Spaccanapoli. Peaceful courtyard and rooftop terrace.

luxury.name: Grand Hotel Vesuvio
luxury.desc: Historic 5-star landmark on the Santa Lucia waterfront. Gulf and Vesuvius views, rooftop gourmet dining.

luxury.name: Romeo Hotel
luxury.desc: Contemporary 5-star by Kenzo Tange with rooftop infinity pool. Michelin-starred restaurant and art collection.

luxury.name: Palazzo Caracciolo Napoli
luxury.desc: Elegant 4-star in a 16th-century palazzo near Via Toledo. Classic meets modern with refined atmosphere.

donts: Don't call it a 'holiday' in Naples - you're visiting a living, breathing, chaotic city. Embrace it.
Don't eat pizza with a knife and fork - fold and eat with your hands like a Neapolitan
Don't wander the Spanish Quarters with valuables on display - it's busy and thieves are quick
Don't skip the archaeological museum - it's where the best Pompeii artefacts live
naples.transport: Naples is walkable but hilly - wear comfortable shoes. The Metro is limited but improving. Funiculars connect the lower city to the Vomero hill - use them, they're fun. Taxis are cheap but agree on price first. The Circumvesuviana train runs to Pompeii, Herculaneum, and Sorrento.

fiestas.desc: elaborate religious processions through the Spaccanapoli street.

fiestas.m: September 19 and first Saturday in May
fiestas.desc: the miracle of the saint's blood liquefying.
the Via San Gregorio Armeno is famous for handcrafted nativity scenes.

attractions.name: Pompeii Archaeological Site
attractions.desc: A Roman city frozen in time by Vesuvius. Allow a full day.

attractions.name: National Archaeological Museum
attractions.desc: The world's finest collection of Roman and Greek artefacts.

attractions.name: Spaccanapoli
attractions.desc: The ancient street splits the old town. The heart of Naples.

attractions.name: Veiled Christ (Cappella Sansevero)
attractions.desc: The most breathtaking marble sculpture you will ever see.

attractions.name: Castel dell'Ovo
attractions.desc: Free castle on the seafront. Sunset views are incredible.

attractions.name: Pizza Pilgrimage (Da Michele/Sorbillo)
attractions.desc: Two legendary pizzerias. The queue is part of the experience.

attractions.name: Herculaneum
attractions.desc: Better preserved than Pompeii. Smaller but more intimate.

attractions.name: Via Toledo and Spanish Quarter
attractions.desc: The beating heart of Neapolitan daily life. Chaotic and brilliant.

day_trips.name: Capri Island
day_trips.tip: Go early, avoid the August crowds. Blue Grotto is worth the extra fee.

day_trips.name: Amalfi Coast (Positano)
day_trips.tip: Take the ferry from Naples - the bus journey is beautiful but nauseating

day_trips.name: Vesuvius Crater Hike
day_trips.tip: Start early before the heat. You can see Naples and the whole bay from the top

day_trips.name: Caserta Palace
day_trips.tip: The Versailles of Italy. The royal palace is extraordinary and usually quiet

airports.name: Naples Capodichino
airports.buses: Alibus shuttle €5 one-way, 15-35 min to city centre (Piazza Garibaldi/Molo Beverello), runs 05:30-midnight every 20-30 min
airports.train: No direct metro. Take Alibus (€5) to Napoli Centrale then Circumvesuviana for regional destinations
airports.taxi: Fixed fare €21-€28.50 depending on zone, 15-30 min; shared taxi option €6 pp

airport.tips: Alibus €5 is best value. Taxis operate on fixed fares (confirm before boarding). Shared taxis €6/person between airport/station/port.

attractionPrices.name: Pompeii
attractionPrices.notes: €20 express, €25 plus (suburban villas). Free 1st Sun. EU 18-25 €2. Daily limit 20,000.

attractionPrices.name: National Archaeological Museum
attractionPrices.notes: Valid 2 consecutive days. Free 1st Sun, Apr 25, Jun 2, Nov 4. Closed Tuesdays.

attractionPrices.name: Veiled Christ (Cappella Sansevero)
attractionPrices.notes: €8 ages 18-26, €6 ages 10-17. Under 9 free. Audio guide €3.50. Book online.

attractionPrices.name: Castel dell'Ovo
attractionPrices.notes: Free entry. Spectacular views of Gulf of Naples and Vesuvius.

attractionPrices.name: Catacombs of San Gennaro
attractionPrices.notes: Guided tour included (hourly). €6 ages 6-17. Closed Wednesdays. Valid 12 months.

attractionPrices.name: Spaccanapoli
attractionPrices.notes: Free open-air street through historic centre. Free walking tours available.

dayTrips.name: Pompeii
dayTrips.description: Roman city frozen in 79 AD — forum, amphitheatre, thermal baths, plaster casts.
dayTrips.transport: Circumvesuviana from Napoli Garibaldi to Pompei Scavi, €3.30, 35 min. Station 50m from entrance.

dayTrips.name: Herculaneum
dayTrips.description: Smaller but better-preserved. Wooden structures, vivid mosaics, far fewer crowds.
dayTrips.transport: Circumvesuviana to Ercolano Scavi, €2.80, 20 min. 5-min walk to entrance.

dayTrips.name: Amalfi Coast
dayTrips.description: Positano, Amalfi, Ravello — dramatic cliffs, pastel villages, turquoise water.
dayTrips.transport: Ferry from Molo Beverello €15-€25, 1-2 hr (Apr-Oct). Or Circumvesuviana to Sorrento €4 then SITA bus.

dayTrips.name: Capri
dayTrips.description: Blue Grotto, Monte Solaro chairlift, Faraglioni, Villa Jovis.
dayTrips.transport: Hydrofoil from Molo Beverello €22-€28, 50 min. 20+ daily in summer.

dayTrips.name: Caserta Palace
dayTrips.description: 18th-century palace with 1,200 rooms, Versailles-style park with waterfalls.
dayTrips.transport: Regional train from Napoli Centrale to Caserta, €4-€5, 40 min. Station opposite palace.

sarah_knows.to_city: Alibus shuttle (€5, 20min to Piazza Garibaldi). Taxi (€20 fixed to historic centre, €25 to Chiaia)
sarah_knows.best_time: April-June or September-October. July-August is brutally hot and crowded. Naples in December has incredible nativity markets
eating: Pizza Margherita at L'Antica Pizzeria da Michele is the one - 2 options (margherita or marinara), no menu, no debate
Sfogliatella (riccio or frolla) at any good pasticceria - the shell-shaped pastry filled with ricotta
Seafood in Naples is extraordinary - try the frittura di pesce (mixed fried seafood) at the port
Coffee at Caffe Gambrinus - historic and overpriced but a rite of passage
safety: Naples has a reputation but is safe for tourists who keep their wits about them
Spanish Quarter and Piazza Garibaldi are fine by day - avoid empty side streets at night
Emergency: 112 (EU-wide). Local police: 113
Don't let the chaos intimidate you - Neapolitans are incredibly protective of tourists
local_tips: Learn 'Pazzesco!' - it means insane/amazing and Neapolitans love hearing you try
The archaeological museum has the best collection of Pompeii artefacts - better than Pompeii itself
Cash is king in Naples - many small shops and pizzerias don't take cards
Pizza is a lunch dish in Naples, not dinner. Evening pizza is for tourists

amalfi.name: Amalfi

taglines.beach: dramatic cliffs, pastel villages, the most beautiful coastline in Europe
taglines.party: sunset cocktails in Positano, dancing in Praiano, aperitivo with a view
taglines.culture: Amalfi Cathedral, paper mills, a maritime republic's story
taglines.food: fresh anchovies, Amalfi lemons (sfusato), limoncello on every menu
taglines.relax: boat trips, seaside lunches, doing absolutely nothing in paradise
taglines.mix: the Amalfi Coast is Italy's dream coastline. Yes, it's as good as the photos.

amalfi.welcome: The Amalfi Coast is Italy in its most dramatic and seductive form. Vertical villages cling to cliffs above impossibly blue water. Positano cascades down to the sea. Ravello sits in the clouds. Amalfi town was once a maritime superpower. The lemons are the size of your face, the limoncello flows freely, and every meal happens with a view that stops conversation. It is expensive, crowded in summer, and absolutely worth every penny.

budget.name: Bella Napoli Positano
budget.desc: Simple budget-friendly B&B in central Positano. Great value base for exploring the Amalfi Coast.

budget.name: Ammattaino House
budget.desc: Cosy self-catering accommodation with local character. Perfect for independent travellers.

budget.name: La Conca Azzurra
budget.desc: Family-run peaceful hotel on the beach. Traditional Amalfi Coast hospitality with direct sea access.
budget.proximity: On the beach between Amalfi and Positano

mid.name: Hotel Miramalfi
mid.desc: Dramatic cliffside hotel with private beach and breathtaking sea views. Romantic Amalfi coastline setting.

mid.name: Covo dei Saraceni
mid.desc: Modern luxury seafront hotel with rooftop pool on Positano beach. Contemporary Mediterranean charm.
mid.proximity: Seafront, on the beach in Positano centre

mid.name: Hotel Villa Franca
mid.desc: Luxury hotel with panoramic cliffside views and spa. Elegant rooms overlooking the Mediterranean.

luxury.name: Le Sirenuse
luxury.desc: Iconic hotel with mid-century elegance and Michelin-starred dining. The most famous address on the Amalfi Coast.
luxury.proximity: Cliffside overlooking Positano, 5-min walk to centre

luxury.name: Hotel Santa Caterina
luxury.desc: Historic elegant hotel with clifftop gardens and private beach. Timeless Amalfi Coast luxury.

luxury.name: Caruso Belmond
luxury.desc: Historic 11th-century palazzo with breathtaking infinity pool suspended over the coast. The pinnacle of Ravello.
luxury.proximity: Clifftop in Ravello, 30-min drive to coast

donts: Don't rent a car unless you're a very confident driver - the Amalfi road is terrifying
Don't visit in August - the buses are sardine tins, the prices double, the joy halves
Don't skip Ravello - it's higher, quieter, and has the most incredible views on the coast
Don't buy limoncello from a tourist shop - ask your restaurant if they make their own
amalfi.transport: The SITA bus connects all Amalfi Coast towns from Sorrento to Salerno. Buy a 24-hour pass (€10). Ferries connect Positano, Amalfi, and Salerno - faster and more scenic than the bus. Taxis between towns are €30-50. Do not rent a car unless you enjoy terror.

fiestas.desc: historical boat race between Amalfi, Venice, Genoa, and Pisa.
Amalfi's patron saint.
celebrating the famous sfusato lemon.

phrases.meaning: What a spectacle! (you'll say this a lot)

attractions.name: Positano
attractions.desc: The most photographed town on the coast. Even better in person.

attractions.name: Amalfi Cathedral (Duomo)
attractions.desc: Arab-Norman architecture. The cloister is stunning.

attractions.name: Ravello and Villa Rufolo
attractions.desc: Gardens that inspired Wagner. Concert stage over the sea.

attractions.name: Path of the Gods (Sentiero degli Dei)
attractions.desc: Hike between Positano and Praiano. The best coastal walk in Europe.

attractions.name: Emerald Grotto
attractions.desc: Sea cave with an underwater nativity scene. Eerie and beautiful.

attractions.name: Amalfi Lemon Grove Tour
attractions.desc: See where the legendary sfusato lemons grow.

attractions.name: Fiordo di Furore
attractions.desc: A fiord carved into the coast. Tiny beach between cliffs.

attractions.name: Villa Cimbrone (Ravello)
attractions.desc: The Terrace of Infinity. The most romantic viewpoint in Italy.

day_trips.name: Capri
day_trips.tip: Go early, skip the Blue Grotto queue, take the chairlift to Monte Solaro

day_trips.name: Pompeii
day_trips.tip: Combined Amalfi + Pompeii train via Sorrento - doable in one day

day_trips.name: Salerno
day_trips.tip: Underrated city with a beautiful medieval quarter. Ferry from Amalfi is 30min

airports.name: Naples Capodichino
airports.buses: Alibus €5 (20 min) + Circumvesuviana €4.60 to Sorrento (70 min) + SITA bus €2.40 to Positano (50-60 min). Total ~€12, 3-4.5h. Pintour direct airport→Amalfi €20, 2h10.
airports.train: Alibus €5 (20 min) + Circumvesuviana Napoli→Sorrento €4.60 (70 min) + SITA bus to Positano/Amalfi. Total ~€12-15, 3-4.5h.
airports.ferry: Alibus €5 + metro/taxi to Molo Beverello + ferry to Positano/Amalfi €20-25 (1.5h). Total ~€27-32, 2.5-3.5h. Seasonal Apr-Oct.
airports.taxi: Official taxi to Positano €150-170, to Amalfi €170-190. 1.5-2h. Pre-booked private transfer from €130.

airports.name: Salerno Costa d'Amalfi
airports.buses: Airlink bus €6 to Salerno station (40 min) then SITA bus along coast. Total ~€9-11, 1.5-2.5h.
airports.train: Free shuttle to Pontecagnano station + train to Salerno ~€2 (10 min). Then bus/ferry to coast.
airports.ferry: Taxi/transfer to Salerno port (€35-70, 20 min) + ferry to Amalfi €9 (35 min) or Positano ~€12 (50 min). Seasonal Apr-Oct.
airports.taxi: Official taxi: Amalfi €110-160, Positano €140-190. Pre-booked: Amalfi €110-170, Positano €130-180.

airport.tips: NAP has far more flight options year-round. QSR is closer (45 km vs 75 km) but limited seasonal routes. Book transfers in advance Jul-Aug. Cheapest NAP→Positano: Circumvesuviana+SITA bus ~€12.

attractionPrices.name: Amalfi Cathedral
attractionPrices.notes: Church free. Monumental complex (Cloister, Crypt, Basilica) €3-5. Free 1st Sun. No booking needed.

attractionPrices.name: Villa Rufolo
attractionPrices.notes: Ravello gardens. €8 adult, €6 reduced (65+/children 5-12). On-site only.

attractionPrices.name: Villa Cimbrone
attractionPrices.notes: Ravello gardens + Terrazza dell'Infinito. Discount for children under 12. No reservation needed.

attractionPrices.name: Path of the Gods
attractionPrices.notes: Free. Bomerano→Nocelle→Positano, ~7.8km, 3-4h. Best Apr-Jun, Sep-Oct.

attractionPrices.name: Emerald Grotto
attractionPrices.notes: Includes guided boat ride inside. Open daily 9:00-16:00. Visit ~15-20 min.

attractionPrices.name: Amalfi Paper Museum
attractionPrices.notes: Self-guided €4.50, guided €7. Housed in medieval paper mill. ~45 min tour.

attractionPrices.name: Spiaggia Grande
attractionPrices.notes: Public beach free. Sunbed rental €20-50/day. Fornillo Beach quieter alternative.

dayTrips.name: Capri
dayTrips.description: Blue Grotto, Monte Solaro chairlift, Faraglioni, Gardens of Augustus.
dayTrips.transport: Ferry from Positano €23 (30 min) or Amalfi €25 (60 min). Services Apr-Oct.

dayTrips.name: Pompeii
dayTrips.description: Roman city frozen in 79 AD — preserved streets, frescoes, casts.
dayTrips.transport: Circumvesuviana from Sorrento €3.30 (35 min). Entry €20.

dayTrips.name: Naples
dayTrips.description: Archaeology museum, Spaccanapoli, pizza birthplace, Castel dell'Ovo.
dayTrips.transport: Train from Salerno €5 (40-60 min) or Sorrento €4.60 (70 min). Ferry from Sorrento €15 (40 min).

dayTrips.name: Ravello
dayTrips.description: Villa Rufolo, Villa Cimbrone Terrace of Infinity, Ravello Festival concerts.
dayTrips.transport: SITA bus from Amalfi €1.50 (25 min, every 30 min). No ferry access.

dayTrips.name: Sorrento
dayTrips.description: Limoncello tastings, cliffside views, Bay of Naples panorama.
dayTrips.transport: Ferry from Positano €16 (25-55 min). SITA bus from Positano €2.40 (50 min).

sarah_knows.to_city: Curreri bus from Naples airport to Sorrento (€10, 70min) then SITA bus along the coast. Car service (€100+, 90min). Ferry from Naples to Positano/Amalfi (€25, 80min) - seasonal
sarah_knows.best_time: May-June or September. July-August is a festival of crowds. October can be wet but is peaceful
eating: Limoncello was invented on Amalfi - it's a digestif, not a cocktail. Drink it after dinner, ice cold
Scialatielli ai frutti di mare is the local pasta - thick ribbons with seafood
Delizia al limone is a lemon dessert from Amalfi - light, sweet, perfect
Positano restaurant prices are 30% higher than Amalfi town for the same quality
safety: The bus is safe but pickpocketing happens on crowded summer buses
Steps everywhere - good shoes are non-negotiable
Emergency: 112 (EU-wide). Coast Guard for boat emergencies: 1530
local_tips: Book restaurants in Positano weeks ahead in summer - the good ones fill up instantly
The Path of the Gods is free and the most beautiful thing you can do here
Take the ferry between towns, not the bus - it's faster, cooler, and the views are better
Buy limoncello from a family producer, not a souvenir shop - ask at your hotel

cinque_terre.name: Cinque Terre

taglines.beach: tiny rocky coves, turquoise water, cliffside villages
taglines.party: sunset spritz in Monterosso, quiet evening walks through vineyards
taglines.culture: UNESCO villages, dry-stone terraces, a thousand years of history
taglines.food: fresh pesto, anchovies from Monterosso, sciacchetra dessert wine
taglines.relax: this is what doing nothing in Italy looks like. Perfect.
taglines.mix: Cinque Terre is five villages in a row, each more beautiful than the last

cinque_terre.welcome: Cinque Terre is five impossibly colourful fishing villages clinging to the Ligurian coast. Monterosso, Vernazza, Corniglia, Manarola, Riomaggiore - each one postcard-perfect. Linked by walking trails, a train line, and ferries, they are a place where the simple pleasures reign: swimming in turquoise coves, eating pesto in a harbourfront trattoria, and watching the sunset paint the pastel houses gold.

budget.name: Cinque Terre Rooms
budget.desc: Simple elegant rooms in the heart of La Spezia. Comfortable base for exploring Cinque Terre via train.

budget.name: La Giovanna Ostello
budget.desc: Budget holiday home in Corniglia with terrace overlooking the village. Simple authentic accommodation.

budget.name: Hotel Marina
budget.desc: Informal family-run hillside hotel with garden, hot tub with sea views and loaner kayaks.

mid.name: Hotel Porto Roca
mid.desc: Upscale 4-star cliff-top hotel with panoramic sea views and saltwater infinity pool. The most prestigious in Monterosso.
mid.proximity: 5-min walk to town, cliffside above sea

mid.name: La Locanda di Anaita
mid.desc: Small family-run guesthouse with private bathrooms and sea views. Simple, clean and well-priced.

mid.name: Villa Steno
mid.desc: Charming 16-room boutique villa surrounded by lemon and olive trees. Warm personal service and panoramic terrace.

luxury.name: Grand Hotel Porto
luxury.desc: Only 5-star in the area, in a 17th-century former convent. Stunning harbour views, elegant and historic.
luxury.proximity: Steps from ferry dock to Cinque Terre

luxury.name: NH Collection Portofino
luxury.desc: Renzo Piano-designed hotel on stilts over Genoa Old Port. Panoramic harbour views. Note: in Genoa, not Portofino.
luxury.proximity: Genoa old port, 1.5hrs from Cinque Terre

luxury.name: Hotel Margherita
luxury.desc: Modern 3-star on Monterosso main street with bright rooms and peaceful patio. Steps from shops and beaches.

donts: Don't drive - the villages are car-free (mostly). Park in La Spezia and take the train
Don't hike between all five villages in one day - you'll be exhausted and miss everything
Don't expect proper sandy beaches - it's rocks and pebbles, except Monterosso
Don't skip Corniglia because it's 'hard to reach' - the 377 steps are worth it
cinque_terre.transport: The Cinque Terre Express train runs between the five villages (La Spezia to Levanto). Buy a Cinque Terre Card (€18/day) for unlimited trains and hiking trail access. Ferries connect the villages from April-October. The hiking trails are the best way to experience the area.

fiestas.desc: Monterosso celebrates the local lemons with food stalls and parades.
patron saint of Riomaggiore with fireworks.
the nativity scenes in Manarola are world-famous, painted on the hillside in lights.

attractions.name: Vernazza
attractions.desc: The most beautiful of the five villages. Harbour square is stunning.

attractions.name: Monterosso al Mare
attractions.desc: The only village with a proper sandy beach.

attractions.name: Manarola at Sunset
attractions.desc: The classic view you've seen in photos. Even better in real life.

attractions.name: Sentiero Azzurro (Blue Trail)
attractions.desc: The coastal trail between villages. Most scenic walk in Italy.

attractions.name: Corniglia
attractions.desc: High on a cliff. 377 steps up but worth every one for the views.

attractions.name: Riomaggiore
attractions.desc: Colourful houses tumbling to the sea. Best for sunset.

attractions.name: Via dell'Amore
attractions.desc: The 'Lover's Lane' walk between Riomaggiore and Manarola.

attractions.name: Pesto Making Class
attractions.desc: Learn to make authentic pesto with local basil.

day_trips.name: Portovenere
day_trips.tip: Beautiful harbour town south of Cinque Terre. Less crowded, just as beautiful

day_trips.name: Portofino
day_trips.tip: The most glamorous fishing village in Italy. Pricey but stunning

day_trips.name: La Spezia
day_trips.tip: Gateway city with a surprisingly good naval museum and great food market

airports.name: Genoa Cristoforo Colombo
airports.buses: Airlink bus €2 (5 min) to Sestri Ponente station then train (~2h, €10-20). Or Volabus €10 (30 min) to Genoa stations then train (1.25-1.75h, €8-12).
airports.train: Airlink bus €2 (5 min) to Sestri Ponente station, then regional train to Monterosso/La Spezia (1.25-2h, €8-15). Total ~1.5-2.5h, €10-17.

airports.name: Pisa Galileo Galilei
airports.buses: PisaMover €5.90-6.50 (5 min) to Pisa Centrale + train to La Spezia (1-1.5h, €8-12) + CT Express to villages (5-15 min, €4-5).
airports.train: PisaMover €5.90-6.50 to Pisa Centrale + regional train to La Spezia (1-1.5h, €8-12). CT Express to villages. Total ~1.5-2h, €15-25.

airport.tips: Pisa is closer (84 km vs 112 km) and generally cheaper/easier by train. Both require CT Express from La Spezia (€4-5 per ride).

attractionPrices.name: Cinque Terre Treno MS Card (1 day)
attractionPrices.notes: Low season Band A. Unlimited trains La Spezia-Levanto, Via dell'Amore, Blue Trail hiking, park toilets, Wi-Fi. Mid €29.50, High €35. Trekking Card from €7.50.

attractionPrices.name: Monterosso Castle
attractionPrices.notes: Free entry. Castle ruins above old town. Open-air site.

attractionPrices.name: Riomaggiore Castle
attractionPrices.notes: €2 basic. €4 with VR. €5 with Via dell'Amore guided tour. Free with CT Card.

attractionPrices.name: Vernazza Castle (Castello Doria)
attractionPrices.notes: €1.50-2. Cash only. Includes Belforte Tower climb with panoramic harbour views.

attractionPrices.name: Corniglia Hiking Trails
attractionPrices.notes: Free for Alta Via, ridge paths. Sentiero Azzurro requires CT Card.

attractionPrices.name: Church of San Francesco
attractionPrices.notes: Free. 17th-century church in Monterosso.

attractionPrices.name: Beaches (Monterosso)
attractionPrices.notes: Free public access. Sunbed + umbrella €15-25/day.

dayTrips.name: Portofino
dayTrips.description: Pastel fishing village, Piazzetta harbour, designer boutiques, Castello Brown.
dayTrips.transport: Train to Santa Margherita Ligure (1-1.5h, €5-7), then bus #782 (20 min, €3) or seasonal ferry (15 min, €8-10)

dayTrips.name: Genoa
dayTrips.description: Largest medieval old town in Italy, Europe's biggest aquarium, Rolli Palaces (UNESCO).
dayTrips.transport: Direct regional train from Monterosso/Vernazza to Genoa Piazza Principe (1.25-1.75h, €8-12). Hourly.

dayTrips.name: Lerici and Tellaro
dayTrips.description: Gulf of Poets towns — medieval castles, colourful houses, Byron's Cave.
dayTrips.transport: Train to La Spezia (10 min, €4), then ATC bus L/S (30 min, €2.50) to Lerici

dayTrips.name: Pisa
dayTrips.description: Leaning Tower, Piazza dei Miracoli (UNESCO), Baptistery, Cathedral.
dayTrips.transport: Train from La Spezia to Pisa Centrale (1-1.5h, €8-12). Frequent regionals.

dayTrips.name: Lucca
dayTrips.description: Walled Renaissance city, bike-friendly ramparts, Roman amphitheatre, Guinigi Tower.
dayTrips.transport: Train from La Spezia to Lucca via Viareggio/Pisa (1.25-1.75h, €8-12)

sarah_knows.airport: Genoa Cristoforo Colombo Airport (GOA) or Pisa Airport (PSA)
sarah_knows.to_city: Train from Genoa to Monterosso (€12, 1.5hr). Train from Pisa to La Spezia (€10, 1hr). Shuttle from La Spezia station to the villages
sarah_knows.best_time: May-June or September. July-August is uncomfortably crowded. Weekdays are dramatically quieter than weekends
eating: Pesto alla Genovese was born in this region - the basil, pine nuts, and parmesan here are the real deal
Anchovies from Monterosso are famous - eat them marinated, not just on pizza
Sciacchetra is a local dessert wine - sweet, golden, served with cantucci biscuits
Focaccia in Cinque Terre is softer and oilier than the Genovese version
safety: The hiking trails have steep drops - keep to the path, wear proper shoes, carry water
Pickpockets on the Cinque Terre Express train - bag in front always
Emergency: 112 (EU-wide). Hiking trail emergency: 118
local_tips: Buy the Cinque Terre Card for unlimited train travel and trail access - it pays for itself in 2 trips
Book accommodation a season ahead - Cinque Terre has more visitors than beds
The train between villages takes 3-5 minutes. Don't hike everything - pick one or two trail sections
Go swimming at sunrise - the water is calm, the villages are quiet, the light is magic

bologna.name: Bologna

taglines.beach: landlocked - but the Adriatic coast is an hour by train
taglines.party: university city = endless student bars, cheap drinks, late nights
taglines.culture: the oldest university in Europe, medieval towers, porticoed streets
taglines.food: Bologna is the food capital of Italy. Tagliatelle, mortadella, tortellini. Non-negotiable.
taglines.relax: Piazza Maggiore at dusk, arcaded walks, aperitivo on Via del Pratello
taglines.mix: Bologna is the city that knows what 'la dolce vita' actually means

bologna.welcome: Bologna is Italy's best-kept secret. It has the oldest university in the Western world, a medieval centre that rivals any city in Europe, and the best food in a country that prides itself on food. Tagliatelle al ragu was invented here. So was tortellini. So was mortadella. The city is compact, walkable, and its porticoed streets shelter you from rain and sun alike. Bolognesi know they live somewhere special, and they're right.

budget.name: We_Bologna Hostel
budget.desc: Modern hostel (also known as Combo Bologna) in a former church. Bar, restaurant, coworking space and courtyard. Popular mix of travellers and locals.

budget.name: Hotel Commercio
budget.desc: Simple budget hotel in central Bologna. Basic rooms with essential amenities for exploring the city on foot.

budget.name: Panorama
budget.desc: Budget hotel with a rooftop terrace offering panoramic Bologna views. Simple clean rooms in a central location.

mid.name: Hotel Cavour
mid.desc: Well-located 3-star near the Two Towers. Comfortable rooms and a good breakfast buffet.

mid.name: Art Hotel Commercianti
mid.desc: Boutique hotel in a historic building opposite Basilica San Petronio. Medieval charm with modern comfort on Bologna main square.

mid.name: Hotel Corona d'Oro
mid.desc: Elegant 4-star on Via Oberdan in the heart of Bologna. Historic building with classic Italian style and excellent service.

luxury.name: Grand Hotel Majestic Gia Baglioni
luxury.desc: Bologna grandest hotel, a historic 5-star on Via Indipendenza. Opulent interiors, renowned restaurant and impeccable service.

luxury.name: Bologna Art Hotel Fiera
luxury.desc: Modern hotel near Bologna Fiere exhibition centre. Clean comfortable rooms, ideal for business or convention visitors.
luxury.proximity: Near exhibition centre, 20-min bus to centre

luxury.name: Hotel Metropolitan
luxury.desc: Traditional 4-star in central Bologna with elegant rooms and a rooftop terrace. Reliable choice for city breaks.

donts: Don't call it spaghetti bolognese - the dish doesn't exist here. It's tagliatelle al ragu.
Don't skip the Quadrilatero market - it's where Bolognesi have shopped for food for 800 years
Don't underestimate the porticoes - they're UNESCO-listed and the longest covered walkways in the world
Don't visit on a Monday - many museums and restaurants are closed
bologna.transport: Bologna is compact and extremely walkable - the historic centre is one of the largest in Europe but easy to navigate. There is no Metro but buses are good. The train station (Bologna Centrale) is the main north-south hub in Italy. Taxis are affordable.

fiestas.desc: the city's patron saint celebration.
a festival celebrating Bologna's famous sausage.
literature festival in beautiful palazzos.

attractions.name: Piazza Maggiore and Basilica of San Petronio
attractions.desc: The heart of the city. The unfinished facade tells a story of ambition.

attractions.name: Two Towers (Asinelli and Garisenda)
attractions.desc: Climb the Asinelli Tower (498 steps) for panoramic views of the city.

attractions.name: Quadrilatero Market
attractions.desc: The ancient food market. Mortadella, parmesan, tortellini - heaven.

attractions.name: Archiginnasio
attractions.desc: The old university building. The botanical theatre is extraordinary.

attractions.name: Santo Stefano Basilica
attractions.desc: Seven churches in one. The most atmospheric religious complex in Bologna.

attractions.name: Portico Walking Tour
attractions.desc: Walk the 4km portico to San Luca. The longest covered walkway in the world.

attractions.name: Mercato di Mezzo
attractions.desc: Modern food hall in a medieval building. Excellent for lunch.

attractions.name: Pinacoteca Nazionale
attractions.desc: Raphael, Titian, and the Bolognese masters. Underrated museum.

day_trips.name: Modena
day_trips.tip: Home of Ferrari, balsamic vinegar, and Osteria Francescana (seat 50 - good luck)

day_trips.name: Parma
day_trips.tip: Parmigiano, prosciutto, and the most beautiful medieval square in Italy

day_trips.name: Ravenna
day_trips.tip: Byzantine mosaics that are among the most important in the world

day_trips.name: Ferrara
day_trips.tip: Renaissance city with a moated castle. Cycling capital of Italy

airports.name: Bologna Guglielmo Marconi
airports.buses: Bus 944 €4, 20-25 min; Marconi Express 949 €9.80, 25-30 min to Piazza Malpighi
airports.train: Marconi Express monorail €12.80 one-way (€23.30 return), 7 min to Bologna Centrale, runs 5:40am-midnight every 7 min peak

airport.tips: Cheapest is Bus 944 (€4). Marconi Express fastest (7 min). Contactless Pay&Go at train gates. After midnight, Line Q runs.

attractionPrices.name: Two Towers (Asinelli climb)
attractionPrices.notes: 498 steps, no elevator. Book online. Garisenda not climbable.

attractionPrices.name: Piazza Maggiore
attractionPrices.notes: Free, always open. Main square.

attractionPrices.name: Basilica of San Petronio
attractionPrices.notes: Free entry. Chapels €5 adults. Dress code: cover shoulders/knees.

attractionPrices.name: Archiginnasio (Anatomical Theatre)
attractionPrices.notes: €3 basic. Guided €12, audio €10. Free under 12. Closed Sunday.

attractionPrices.name: Fountain of Neptune
attractionPrices.notes: Free outdoor fountain by Piazza Maggiore.

attractionPrices.name: Santo Stefano Complex (Seven Churches)
attractionPrices.notes: Free entry, donations welcome. Small museum €3-5. Closed Mondays.

attractionPrices.name: Bologna Cathedral (Duomo di San Pietro)
attractionPrices.notes: Free entry. Baroque interior.

dayTrips.name: Modena
dayTrips.description: Romanesque Cathedral (UNESCO), Ferrari/Maserati/Lamborghini, balsamic vinegar.
dayTrips.transport: Train from Bologna Centrale 20-35 min, €3.85-€16, every ~20-30 min

dayTrips.name: Parma
dayTrips.description: Parmesan cheese, Prosciutto di Parma, Correggio frescoes, Teatro Farnese.
dayTrips.transport: Train ~50-70 min, €7.35-€21.50. Frequent departures.

dayTrips.name: Ravenna
dayTrips.description: Byzantine mosaics — Basilica di San Vitale, Galla Placidia, Sant'Apollinare Nuovo.
dayTrips.transport: Train ~1h direct, €9-€12. 19-26 direct trains daily.

dayTrips.name: Ferrara
dayTrips.description: Estense Castle, Palazzo dei Diamanti, bike-friendly, cappellacci di zucca.
dayTrips.transport: Train 30-50 min, €4.75-€10, every ~30 min

dayTrips.name: San Marino
dayTrips.description: Oldest republic, three medieval fortresses on Mount Titano, duty-free shopping.
dayTrips.transport: Train Bologna→Rimini (~1h, €10-15) + Bonelli Bus to San Marino (€6, 45 min)

sarah_knows.to_city: Marconi Express monorail to Bologna Centrale (€11, 7min). BLQ bus (€6, 20min). Taxi (€20 fixed, 20min)
sarah_knows.best_time: April-June or September-October. Summer can be very hot and humid. Autumn is truffle season
eating: Tagliatelle al ragu is NOT spaghetti bolognese - it's fresh egg pasta with meat ragu. There is no spaghetti version.
Tortellini in brodo (in broth) is the classic Bolognese dish - especially in winter
Mortadella is NOT bologna (the American version). Try the real thing at the market
Parmigiano Reggiano, Prosciutto di Parma, and Aceto Balsamico Tradizionale all come from nearby
safety: Bologna is very safe. Watch belongings in the market area and on crowded buses
The university district (around Via Zamboni) is lively at night - fine but busy
Emergency: 112 (EU-wide)
local_tips: The secret to Bolognese food: quality ingredients, not complex recipes. The same philosophy applies to everything here
Climb the Asinelli Tower early - the queue builds fast. 498 steps but worth it
Thursday night is student night - Bologna comes alive. Join in
The portico walk to San Luca takes 40 minutes uphill. Do it for the view at the top

como.name: Como

taglines.beach: no beach, but lake swimming off the boat is even better
taglines.party: elegant bars, lakeside aperitivo, casino at Campione
taglines.culture: Romanesque cathedral, silk museums, historic villas
taglines.food: lake fish, risotto, polenta, and the most elegant dining in Lombardy
taglines.relax: lake views, villa gardens, mountain air. This is the antidote to stress.
taglines.mix: Como is where the Lombardy elite escape. You'll understand why in about 10 minutes.

como.welcome: Lake Como is where Italy goes to breathe. The lake is shaped like an inverted Y, framed by Alpine foothills and dotted with villages that seem too beautiful to be real. Como city serves as the gateway, with a lovely medieval centre and a magnificent cathedral. But the real magic is on the water - in Bellagio, Varenna, and the villas that line the shore. George Clooney owns a villa here. You will immediately see why.

budget.name: Ostello Bello Lake Como
budget.desc: Social hostel with buzzing bar, live music and secret garden. Free pasta kitchen and nightly events.

budget.name: Hotel Firenze
budget.desc: Classic 3-star in Como historic centre with old-world charm and rooftop terrace. Family-run steps from the Duomo.

budget.name: La Locanda del Vapore
budget.desc: Boutique B&B in a converted steam laundry with eclectic design. Each room has unique vintage character.

mid.name: Hotel Villa Flori
mid.desc: Elegant 4-star lakeside with Art Nouveau flair and private beach. Early 1900s villa with lush gardens and pool.

mid.name: Palazzo Albricci Peregrini
mid.desc: Luxury boutique in a restored 16th-century palazzo with frescoed ceilings. Spa and rooftop lounge.

mid.name: Albergo Terminus
mid.desc: Historic 4-star from the 1800s in Piazza Volta with grand Art Nouveau lobby. Individually decorated rooms.
mid.proximity: Adjacent to Como Lago station, 5-min to Duomo

luxury.name: Grand Hotel Tremezzo
luxury.desc: Iconic Art Nouveau palace with three pools (one floating on the lake). Michelin-starred dining and Bellagio views.

luxury.name: Villa d'Este
luxury.desc: Legendary 16th-century villa turned 5-star resort. Floating pool, sprawling gardens, hosting royalty since 1873.

luxury.name: Mandarin Oriental Lago di Como
luxury.desc: Ultra-luxury retreat in lakeside villas. Oriental spa and Michelin-starred dining. Discreet five-star sophistication.

donts: Don't arrive without booking - Lake Como in summer sells out months ahead
Don't stay in Como city the whole time - the villages are the real magic
Don't skip a boat tour - seeing the villas from the water is essential
Don't try to drive around the lake - the roads are narrow and parking impossible
como.transport: Trains from Milan to Como (€5-10, 40-60min depending on service). The ferry system is the best way to explore the lake - buy a day pass (€18-30). The C30 bus runs up the western shore. Taxis between villages are expensive. Walking is best in the small towns.

fiestas.desc: medieval reenactment in Como city.
lake fish festivals in various villages.
Como and Bellagio have festive markets that are beautiful.

phrases.phrase: Un giro in barca, per favore

attractions.name: Bellagio
attractions.desc: The 'Pearl of the Lake'. Cobbled streets, stunning views, elegant shops.

attractions.name: Varenna
attractions.desc: Quieter than Bellagio, equally beautiful. The lakeside promenade is perfect.

attractions.name: Villa del Balbianello
attractions.desc: Star Wars filming location. The gardens are extraordinary.

attractions.name: Como Cathedral (Duomo)
attractions.desc: Marble gothic cathedral. Impressive for a city this size.

attractions.name: Funicular to Brunate
attractions.desc: Panoramic view over the lake from the mountain village above Como.

attractions.name: Villa Carlotta
attractions.desc: Botanical gardens and art collection in Tremezzo. Stunning azaleas.

attractions.name: Menaggio
attractions.desc: Lively town on the western shore. Great restaurants and lake swim spots.

attractions.name: Isola Comacina
attractions.desc: The only island on Lake Como. Tiny, historic, worth the boat trip.

day_trips.name: Switzerland (Lugano)
day_trips.tip: Bring your passport! Cross-border town is 30 minutes by train. Completely different vibe

day_trips.name: Lake Garda
day_trips.tip: Italy's largest lake. Different character to Como - more resort, more fun

day_trips.name: Milan
day_trips.tip: Fast train from Como to Milan in 40 minutes. Easy day trip

airports.name: Milan Malpensa
airports.train: Malpensa Exp to Saronno + regional to Como Nord Lago, 60 min, ~€12

airports.name: Milan Linate
airports.train: Not applicable; bus to Stazione Centrale then train to Como

airports.name: Bergamo Orio al Serio
airports.train: Bus to Bergamo station then train to Como

airport.tips: MXP is most practical. Flibco bus is cheapest direct option to Como. Book online.

attractionPrices.name: Como Cathedral
attractionPrices.notes: Free entry. Roof climb ~€7. Modest dress required.

attractionPrices.name: Villa del Balbianello
attractionPrices.notes: Park only €15, villa+guided tour €25. Closed Mon & Wed. Mandatory online booking weeks ahead.

attractionPrices.name: Villa Carlotta
attractionPrices.notes: €17.50 adults, €15 seniors, €12 students, €7 children 6-18. Best Apr-May for azalea bloom.

attractionPrices.name: Funicular to Brunate
attractionPrices.notes: €7.20 round trip, €3.60 single. Kids under 1.1m free. Tickets at station only. 7 min ride.

attractionPrices.name: Monte Bisbino
attractionPrices.notes: Free hiking summit 1,325m. Start from Cernobbio, 3-4 hr hike or drive up.

attractionPrices.name: Volta Temple
attractionPrices.notes: €6 adults, €4 reduced. Free on Intl Museum Day (May 18). Como Art Pass for 3 city museums €16.

dayTrips.name: Bellagio
dayTrips.description: Pearl of the Lake at Como Y-fork. Villa Melzi gardens (€8), cobbled lanes, designer boutiques.
dayTrips.transport: Ferry from Como (2h slow/45min fast, €12.40/€18.80). Central lake day pass €15.20.

dayTrips.name: Varenna
dayTrips.description: Pastel fishing village, Passeggiata degli Innamorati, Villa Monastero gardens (€10).
dayTrips.transport: Direct train from Milan Centrale (1h, €7.40). Ferry from Bellagio (15 min, €4.60).

dayTrips.name: Lugano (Switzerland)
dayTrips.description: Lake Lugano, Monte San Salvatore funicular, Swiss chocolate, high-end shopping.
dayTrips.transport: Direct train from Milan Centrale (1h 15min, ~€15). Or from Como by train (30 min).

dayTrips.name: Milan
dayTrips.description: Duomo rooftop, The Last Supper, La Scala, Galleria Vittorio Emanuele II, Navigli.
dayTrips.transport: Train from Como San Giovanni (40 min, €5.20) or Varenna (1h, €7.40). Every 30-60 min.

dayTrips.name: Bergamo
dayTrips.description: Medieval Citta Alta, Piazza Vecchia, Venetian walls (UNESCO), funicular to San Vigilio.
dayTrips.transport: Direct train from Milan Centrale (48 min, €6). Hourly.

sarah_knows.airport: Milan Malpensa (MXP) or Linate (LIN). Both 60-90 minutes from Como
sarah_knows.to_city: Train from Milan Cadorna (€5, 60min). Malpensa Express to Como (€15, 70min). Car service from Milan (€80, 45min)
sarah_knows.best_time: May-June or September. July-August is peak season with crowds and heat. October has golden autumn colours
eating: Misultini (dried lake fish) are a Como specialty - an acquired taste but try it
Risotto with perch (risotto con pesce persico) is the classic lake dish
Polenta is more common here than pasta - it's the Alpine influence
Local wines like Sassella and Inferno from nearby Valtellina are excellent
safety: Lake Como is extremely safe. Usual vigilance with valuables in crowded areas
Emergency: 112 (EU-wide). Lake rescue: 115
local_tips: Don't bother with a car - the ferry pass is the best way to see the lake
Book dinner restaurants in Bellagio weeks ahead - the lakeside tables go instantly
