Swim off the boat, not from the shore - most of the shoreline is private
Visit the villas early (opening time) or late (2 hours before closing) for fewer crowds

turin.name: Turin

taglines.beach: landlocked - but the Alps are right there for hikes
taglines.party: mole, movies, aperitivo on the Po. Elegant rather than wild.
taglines.culture: Egyptian Museum, Savoy palaces, the Shroud of Turin
taglines.food: chocolate, truffles, vermouth, and the best coffee in Italy
taglines.relax: arcaded streets, cafe culture, mountain views at sunset
taglines.mix: Turin is Italy's most elegant city - and nobody in the north tells you about it

turin.welcome: Turin is Italy's most underrated city. Once the capital of the Kingdom of Italy, it is a city of arcaded boulevards, grand piazzas, and the most elegant cafes in the country. The Egyptian Museum is second only to Cairo's. The food is extraordinary - chocolate, truffles, vermouth, and agnolotti. The Alps provide a dramatic backdrop. Turin is sophisticated, cultured, and waiting to be discovered.

budget.name: Hostel Turin
budget.desc: Budget-friendly hostel with dorms and private rooms. Shared kitchen, lounge and garden terrace.

budget.name: Hotel Dogana Vecchia
budget.desc: Turin oldest hotel (300+ years) with antique and modern decor. Stone floors and marble staircases.

budget.name: B&B Le Chiocciole
budget.desc: Cozy B&B in Turin. Note: may not have direct Booking.com listing - uses search fallback.

mid.name: Hotel Principi di Piemonte
mid.desc: Iconic 5-star Rationalist landmark from the 1930s. Panoramic city and Alps views, indoor pool and spa.

mid.name: NH Collection Torino Santo Stefano
mid.desc: Modern 4-star in a Gabetti-designed building. Rooftop terrace with panoramic city views.

mid.name: Hotel Liberty
mid.desc: Charming 3-star with elegant furnishings, spacious rooms and small spa. Peaceful retreat near the centre.

luxury.name: Grand Hotel Sitea
luxury.desc: Luxury 5-star with Michelin-starred restaurant and marble bathrooms. Behind Piazza San Carlo.

luxury.name: Principale di Piemonte
luxury.desc: Hotel in central Turin. Note: may not have direct Booking.com listing - uses search fallback.

luxury.name: Le Meridien Lingotto

donts: Don't miss the Egyptian Museum - it's the best outside Cairo and most tourists skip it
Don't skip a cafe in one of Turin's historic cafes - Caffe San Carlo or Caffe Torino
Don't expect wild nightlife - Turin is elegant and intellectual, not a club city
Don't forget to buy chocolate - Gianduiotto is the local specialty
turin.transport: Turin is a grid city - very easy to navigate. The Metro has one line but covers the main train station and centre. Trams and buses fill the rest. The city is very walkable, especially the arcaded streets. Buy a 24-hour GTT pass for unlimited transport.

fiestas.desc: chocolate festival in Piazza Vittorio.
Slow Food's flagship event.
patron saint with fireworks on the Po River.

phrases.meaning: A bicerin (Turin's famous hot drink), please

attractions.name: Mole Antonelliana and Cinema Museum
attractions.desc: Turin's landmark. The lift goes to the top. The cinema museum inside is world-class.

attractions.name: Egyptian Museum (Museo Egizio)
attractions.desc: Second only to Cairo. Give it 3 hours minimum. The statues are extraordinary.

attractions.name: Piazza San Carlo
attractions.desc: Turin's 'drawing room'. Two Baroque churches and the best cafes.

attractions.name: Piazza Castello and Royal Palace
attractions.desc: The Savoy residence. The Chapel of the Holy Shroud is inside.

attractions.name: Porta Palazzo Market
attractions.desc: Europe's largest outdoor market. Every food you can imagine.

attractions.name: Basilica of Superga
attractions.desc: On the hill above Turin. Views over the city and Alps are breathtaking.

attractions.name: Roman Theatre and Palatine Tower
attractions.desc: Roman ruins integrated into the city. Free to visit.

attractions.name: Valentino Park
attractions.desc: Lovely park along the Po. Medieval castle inside. Sunday afternoon spot.

day_trips.name: Alps (Via Lattea Ski Area)
day_trips.tip: Winter skiing, summer hiking. 90 minutes from Turin to Sestriere

day_trips.name: Langhe Wine Region
day_trips.tip: Barolo and Barbaresco vineyards. Truffle hunting in autumn. Italy's best wine region

day_trips.name: Aosta Valley
day_trips.tip: Roman ruins, medieval castles, Alpine views. Completely different world

day_trips.name: Alba and Asti
day_trips.tip: White truffle capital of the world. Go in autumn for the truffle fair

airports.name: Turin Caselle
airports.buses: Arriva Italia €7.50 (€8.50 on board), 45-50 min to Porta Nuova/Porta Susa; Flibco from €3.99 online, 25-30 min direct
airports.train: SFM4/6/7 regional €3.60, 30-35 min to Porta Susa, every 30 min weekdays

airport.tips: Train is cheapest/fastest. Flibco bus best value at €3.99 online. Torino+Piemonte Card includes unlimited public transport.

attractionPrices.name: Mole Antonelliana (Cinema Museum)
attractionPrices.notes: Museum €18, panoramic lift €9, combined €23. Free under-5s. No regular free day.

attractionPrices.name: Egyptian Museum
attractionPrices.notes: €18 full, €3 students (15-18), €1 juniors (6-14). Online booking mandatory. Free on your birthday.

attractionPrices.name: Palazzo Reale (Royal Palace)
attractionPrices.notes: €15 full (Tues-Sun). Monday-only €10. €2 reduced (18-25). Free 1st Sun. Closed Mondays.

attractionPrices.name: Palazzo Madama
attractionPrices.notes: €10 full, €8 reduced, €5 garden only. Free 1st Sun. Houses Museo Civico d'Arte Antica.

attractionPrices.name: Cathedral + Shroud of Turin
attractionPrices.notes: Cathedral free. Shroud replica on display; original rare expositions only.

attractionPrices.name: Valentino Park
attractionPrices.notes: Free public park along Po River. Medieval castle, botanical garden.

dayTrips.name: Alba & Barolo Wine Region
dayTrips.description: UNESCO Langhe hills, Barolo and Barbaresco vineyards, white truffle fair (autumn).
dayTrips.transport: Train Porta Nuova/Susa to Alba €6-11, 1h20min. Limited bus to villages. Car recommended.

dayTrips.name: Aosta Valley
dayTrips.description: Roman ruins (Arch of Augustus, theatre), Alpine backdrop, Mont Blanc/Matterhorn gateway.
dayTrips.transport: Train Porta Nuova to Aosta €10-15, ~2h. Hourly departures.

dayTrips.name: Sacra di San Michele
dayTrips.description: Mountaintop abbey (inspiration for The Name of the Rose), 960m up, panoramic views.
dayTrips.transport: Train+bus €6.30 to Avigliana then bus (seasonal Sat/Sun only, Apr-Oct). Taxi from Avigliana €25.

dayTrips.name: Vercelli
dayTrips.description: Rice paddies, Basilica di Sant'Andrea (Romanesque-Gothic), Vercelli Book manuscript.
dayTrips.transport: Train from Porta Nuova/Susa from €7.70, 35-50 min. ~25 trains daily.

dayTrips.name: Langhe Hills (UNESCO)
dayTrips.description: Vineyards, medieval villages (Barolo, La Morra, Neive), wine tastings, cycling.
dayTrips.transport: Train to Alba €6-11, 1h10min. Car or organised tour strongly recommended.

sarah_knows.to_city: SADEM bus from airport to Porta Susa (€7, 40min). Taxi (€35 fixed, 30min)
sarah_knows.best_time: April-June or September-October. Winter for skiing in the Alps. Summer is hot but less crowded than other Italian cities
eating: Gianduia (chocolate-hazelnut spread) was invented in Turin - buy the real thing, not Nutella
Bicerin is Turin's signature drink - espresso, chocolate, cream layered in a small glass
Agnolotti del plin are tiny pasta parcels filled with meat - a local specialty
Vermouth was invented in Turin - visit Carpano or Cinzano for the history
safety: Turin is one of Italy's safest cities. Standard precautions apply at Porta Nuova station at night
Emergency: 112 (EU-wide)
local_tips: The arcaded streets (portici) stretch 18 kilometres - Turin has the longest covered walkways
Egyptian Museum tickets should be booked online in advance to skip the queue
Turin has the best cafe culture in Italy - sit down at Caffe Torino or Caffe San Carlo
The Museo dell'Automobile is brilliant even if you don't care about cars - the building is stunning
